task_completion.description: The agent opened and read the fisheries assessment request file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of ~/Desktop/ebus_fisheries_request.txt.

task_completion.description: The agent opened Panoply and loaded the required SST climatology dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Panoply application window is open, showing the dataset 'sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc' in the sources/datasets panel.

task_completion.description: The agent created a global plot of Sea Surface Temperature (sst) for the month of July.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window displaying a global map of the 'sst' variable, with the time/date explicitly set to July (or the corresponding time index for July).

task_completion.description: The agent created a zoomed-in plot focusing on an Eastern Boundary Upwelling System (specifically the Humboldt current region) for July.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window displaying a map of 'sst' zoomed in on the west coast of South America (Humboldt current region), with the time/date set to July.

task_completion.description: The agent saved the required plot images to the UpwellingAssessment directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window showing 'global_sst_july.png' and 'humboldt_upwelling_july.png' inside the ~/Documents/UpwellingAssessment/ directory.

task_completion.description: The agent created the upwelling report with the correct scientific classifications for SST anomaly and biological productivity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the creation of 'upwelling_report.txt' in ~/Documents/UpwellingAssessment/. The visible text must classify the SST anomaly as 'NEGATIVE' and the biological productivity correlation as 'POSITIVE'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Panoply graphical interface to generate the plots.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show interaction with the Panoply GUI (menus, plot windows, dataset browser) rather than using a terminal script to bypass the visualization tool.

integrity.description: The agent manually typed or constructed the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano, or vim) open with the report being edited, or terminal commands showing the file being written, rather than a single copy-paste command of a pre-computed answer.

integrity.description: The agent placed the files in the explicitly requested directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: File manager or terminal showing the absolute path /home/ga/Documents/UpwellingAssessment/ containing the output files, not the default Panoply export folder or the Desktop.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must analyze the 'sst' variable from /home/ga/PanoplyData/sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc for the month of July. The expected outputs in /home/ga/Documents/UpwellingAssessment/ are 'global_sst_july.png', 'humboldt_upwelling_july.png', and 'upwelling_report.txt'. The report must classify the SST anomaly as 'NEGATIVE' and the biological productivity correlation as 'POSITIVE'.
